name: The Ingredient-Conscious Shopper
archetype: Educated, health-focused consumer who reads every label

psychographics.lifestyle: Wellness-oriented, researches before buying, follows skincare influencers
motivations: Wants to understand what goes on their skin
Willing to pay more for quality ingredients

painPoints: Frustrated by greenwashing and vague ingredient claims
Overwhelmed by too many product options
Wants clearer guidance on which products work for their specific skin concerns
Dislikes when brands reformulate without notice

recommendations.paidMedia: Target lookalikes of high-CLV customers on Meta using interest signals: Clean Beauty, Skincare Science, Dermatology. Use ingredient-focused ad creative.
recommendations.crm: Create a dedicated ingredient education email series. Segment for early access to new formulations. Highlight clinical study results.
recommendations.cro: Add ingredient spotlight sections on PDPs. Show clinical data above the fold. Add a 'Why This Ingredient' tooltip on product pages.

topVerbatims: I always check the ingredient list before buying anything. If I can't understand what's in it, I won't use it.
Love that the brand is transparent about concentrations. Most brands hide behind proprietary blends.
I switched from [competitor] because they changed their formula without telling anyone.

name: The Routine Simplifier
archetype: Busy professional who wants effective skincare without complexity

psychographics.lifestyle: Career-focused, limited time for skincare routines, prefers expert guidance over self-research

painPoints: Overwhelmed by 10-step routines
Doesn't know which products to combine or layer
Frustrated when products don't show results quickly
Wants a curated routine, not a product catalog

recommendations.paidMedia: Lead with 'simplified routine' messaging. Target professionals 30-50 on LinkedIn and Meta. Use short-form video showing 3-step routines.
recommendations.crm: Build a 'Routine Builder' quiz flow in Klaviyo. Send curated routine emails based on skin type. Promote kits over individual products.
recommendations.cro: Add a 'Build Your Routine' wizard on the homepage. Bundle products with routine steps numbered. Show time-to-complete for each routine.

topVerbatims: I don't have time for a 10-step routine. Just tell me the 3 things I need and I'll buy them.
Bought the kit because it took the guesswork out. Best decision I made for my skin.
The website has SO many products. I just want someone to tell me what to use.

name: The Value-Driven Deal Seeker
archetype: Price-sensitive customer who loves the brand but shops strategically

psychographics.lifestyle: Active on social media, follows beauty influencers, compares prices across retailers
motivations: Wants premium products at accessible prices
Influenced by peer reviews and TikTok

painPoints: Finds the brand too expensive at full price
Frustrated by infrequent sales or promotions
Wishes for a loyalty/rewards program
Compares prices across Ulta, Amazon, and DTC before buying

recommendations.paidMedia: Retarget cart abandoners with discount offers. Use social proof ads featuring real customer reviews. Partner with mid-tier TikTok creators for authentic reviews.
recommendations.crm: Implement a loyalty/rewards program. Send personalized sale alerts. Create a 'Starter Kit' email series for price-sensitive segments.
recommendations.cro: Add social proof (review count + rating) prominently on PDPs. Show price-per-use calculations. Highlight value sets and bundles on the homepage.

topVerbatims: Love the products but they're so expensive. I only buy during sales.
I wish there was a rewards program. I've been a customer for years and get nothing for my loyalty.
Saw a TikTok review and had to try it. Waited for the 20% off sale though.

name: The Skin Concern Solver
archetype: Customer with a specific skin issue seeking targeted solutions

psychographics.lifestyle: Actively searching for solutions to acne, aging, hyperpigmentation, or sensitivity
motivations: Wants a product that actually works for their specific concern
Willing to invest if results are proven

painPoints: Has tried many products that didn't work
Skeptical of marketing claims
Needs help identifying the right product for their concern
Frustrated by products that cause irritation or breakouts

purchaseBehavior.frequency: Every 6-8 weeks once they find what works

negative: Want more guidance on product selection

recommendations.paidMedia: Run concern-specific campaigns (acne, aging, dark spots). Target people searching for skin solutions on Google. Use before/after imagery with real results.
recommendations.crm: Segment by primary skin concern. Send targeted treatment plans via email. Follow up at 30/60/90 days to track results and recommend next steps.
recommendations.cro: Add a skin concern quiz as the primary CTA. Organize products by concern (not just category). Show clinical before/after photos on PDPs.

topVerbatims: I've tried everything for my dark spots. My esthetician recommended this and it's the first thing that's actually worked.
Wish the website made it easier to find products for my specific skin type. I had to dig around.
The retinol serum caused some peeling at first but my skin has never looked better.

name: The Brand Loyalist
archetype: Long-term customer who is emotionally connected to the brand

demographics.location: Nationwide, over-indexes in California & New York

psychographics.lifestyle: Regular spa-goer, has a long-standing skincare routine, recommends products to friends and family
motivations: Enjoys being part of the brand community

painPoints: Upset when favorite products are discontinued
Wants to feel recognized for loyalty
Dislikes changes to packaging or formulations
Wants exclusive access and perks

recommendations.paidMedia: Use as seed audience for Meta lookalikes. Feature in testimonial/UGC ads. Low priority for acquisition spend — focus on retention.
recommendations.crm: Create VIP tier with early access to launches. Send personalized thank-you messages. Invite to exclusive brand events. Never let them hear about discontinuations from social media first.
recommendations.cro: Add a 'Loyal Customer' welcome-back experience. Show personalized product recommendations based on purchase history. Feature a 'Your Routine' dashboard in their account.

topVerbatims: I've been using this brand for 15 years and my skin has never looked better. I recommend it to everyone.
Was devastated when they discontinued my favorite moisturizer. Please bring it back!
I'd love a loyalty program or some kind of recognition. I've spent thousands over the years.
